pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 16 clones from https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp.git to /opt/openice/mdpnp; confirmed with actual OpenICE repository
export_result.sh line 12 defines REPORT_PATH; setup_task.sh line 21 references the same path
task.json line 19 specifies min_report_bytes: 500; verifier.py line 51 checks minimum 100 bytes; 500 bytes is appropriate for a 5-section IEC 62304 compliance report
task.json lines 20-26 lists exactly 5 required sections
Verified against OpenICE settings.gradle which defines 29 modules via include statements
Verified against OpenICE build.gradle files listing dependencies including: log4j, slf4j, spring, guava, hapi, mongodb, aws-sdk, junit, etc.
OpenICE uses RTI Connext DDS confirmed by: data-types/x73-idl-rti-dds module, nddsjava.jar artifact reference, RTI_LICENSE_FILE environment variables
OpenICE uses Gradle 7.6 (gradle-wrapper.properties distributionUrl) and Java 17 (build.gradle JAVA_VERSION_SOURCE = 17)
Task description requires at least 3 identified dependencies classified as High, Medium, or Low risk based on IEC 62304 SOUP (Software of Unknown Pedigree) framework
Confirmed in OpenICE repository: data-types/x73-idl-rti-dds module uses nddsjava.jar and rtiddsgen2.jar artifacts from RTI
build.gradle line 12: JAVA_VERSION_SOURCE = 17; setup_openice.sh line 10 sets JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64

privileged_info_summary: OpenICE is a real, publicly available medical device integration platform from the MD PnP program (https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp). The repository contains 29 modules defined in settings.gradle, including device adapters and demonstration applications. The build system uses Gradle 7.6 with Java 17. The platform uses RTI Connext DDS middleware for medical device communication. Major third-party dependencies include Spring Framework, Apache Logging (log4j), SLF4J, HL7 HAPI, MongoDB drivers, and AWS SDK. These dependencies should be classified as part of SOUP (Software of Unknown Pedigree) per IEC 62304 medical device software lifecycle standards.
